entries.preparationInstructions: Informed consent. Mark the lesion. Clean and drape the area. Infiltrate local anesthetic.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Keep wound clean and dry for 48 hours. Apply antibiotic ointment as directed. Follow up for suture removal in 7-10 days. Watch for signs of infection.
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Observe for 30 minutes post-procedure for bleeding. Wound check at 48 hours.

entries.preparationInstructions: Informed consent. Assess abscess size and depth. Clean the area. Local anesthesia (field block around abscess).
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Daily dressing changes. Warm compresses. Oral antibiotics as prescribed. Follow up in 2-3 days for wound review. Watch for recurrence.
contraindications: Overlying critical structures without imaging guidance
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Observe for 30 minutes. Ensure packing is secure. Daily dressing review.

entries.preparationInstructions: Informed consent. Verify correct side. Clean the injection site with antiseptic. Topical anesthetic if needed.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Rest the joint for 24-48 hours. Apply ice if needed. Avoid strenuous activity for 48 hours. Report any increased swelling, redness, or fever.
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Observe for 15 minutes post-injection. Assess joint mobility.

entries.preparationInstructions: Bowel preparation with polyethylene glycol (PEG) solution the day before. Clear liquid diet for 24 hours. NPO for 6-8 hours. Inform about blood thinners and diabetes medications.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Rest for 2-4 hours post-sedation. Start with clear liquids, advance diet as tolerated. Do not drive for 24 hours. Report bleeding, severe abdominal pain, or fever immediately.
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor in recovery for 1-2 hours. Check vitals every 15 minutes. Assess for abdominal pain and bleeding.

entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours. Inform about blood thinners and diabetes medications. Local pharyngeal anesthesia spray.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: NPO for 1-2 hours post-procedure until gag reflex returns. Start with sips of water, then advance diet. Do not drive for 24 hours if sedated. Report any bleeding or severe pain.
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor in recovery for 30-60 minutes. Check gag reflex before oral intake. Assess for bleeding or pain.

entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours. Check renal function and coagulation profile. Ensure IV access. Shave groin/wrist access site. Pre-medication with antiplatelet agents if indicated. Hydration protocol for renal protection.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Bed rest for 4-6 hours (femoral access) or 2-4 hours (radial access). Monitor access site for bleeding/hematoma. Adequate hydration to flush contrast. Report any chest pain, access site swelling, or numbness in limb.
preProcedureRequirements: CBC, renal function, coagulation profile, blood grouping
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Continuous cardiac monitoring for 4-6 hours. Check access site every 15 minutes for first 2 hours. Monitor renal function post-contrast.

entries.anesthesiaType: General Anesthesia / Spinal Anesthesia / Paracervical Block
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours. Pre-anesthesia assessment. Urine pregnancy test (if applicable). Cervical priming with misoprostol if indicated.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Expect mild cramping and spotting for 1-2 weeks. Use sanitary pads (no tampons). Avoid intercourse for 2 weeks. Report heavy bleeding (soaking more than one pad per hour), fever, or foul-smelling discharge.
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor in recovery for 2-4 hours. Check vitals and vaginal bleeding every 15 minutes. Assess uterine tone.

entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours. Pre-anesthesia assessment. Verify correct side (surgical site marking). MRI review. Inform about blood thinners.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Ice application and elevation of the leg. Knee immobilizer or crepe bandage. Start physiotherapy as directed. Partial weight bearing with crutches. Follow up in 5-7 days for suture removal. Report excessive swelling, fever, or calf pain.
risksAndComplications: Nerve injury (infrapatellar branch of saphenous nerve)
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor in recovery for 2-4 hours. Check neurovascular status of limb. Assess for compartment syndrome. Pain management.

entries.anesthesiaType: Conscious Sedation with Local Anesthesia (topical lidocaine)
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours. Pre-procedure spirometry if possible. Check coagulation profile. IV access. Topical anesthesia to airway.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: NPO for 2 hours until gag reflex returns. Semi-upright position. Mild hemoptysis and sore throat expected. Do not drive for 24 hours if sedated. Report excessive bleeding, breathing difficulty, or fever.
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Continuous pulse oximetry for 2 hours. Chest X-ray post-procedure (if transbronchial biopsy done). Monitor for pneumothorax and bleeding.

entries.anesthesiaType: Local Anesthesia (intraurethral lidocaine gel) / Spinal Anesthesia
entries.preparationInstructions: Empty bladder before procedure. Urine culture (to rule out active UTI). Prophylactic antibiotics if indicated. Informed consent.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Drink plenty of fluids to flush the bladder. Mild burning during urination and slight hematuria expected for 24-48 hours. Report persistent bleeding, inability to urinate, fever, or severe pain.
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Observe for 30-60 minutes. Ensure patient can void. Assess for hematuria.

entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 4-6 hours. Dilate pupil with mydriatic drops. Pre-operative biometry for IOL power calculation. Prophylactic antibiotic eye drops day before surgery.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Wear protective eye shield. Use prescribed eye drops (antibiotic + steroid + NSAID). Do not rub or press the eye. Avoid bending, lifting heavy objects, and swimming for 2-4 weeks. Follow up next day, then 1 week, and 6 weeks.
contraindications: Uncontrolled diabetes with active proliferative retinopathy
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Check intraocular pressure 2-4 hours post-surgery. Assess wound integrity. First post-operative visit next day.

entries.facilityRequired: Operation Theatre with neonatal resuscitation facility
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours (elective). Blood grouping and cross-match. Pre-anesthesia assessment. Antacid prophylaxis. Urinary catheter. IV access with wide-bore cannula.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Early mobilization (within 12-24 hours). Start oral fluids after 6 hours. Breastfeeding to be initiated within 1 hour. Wound care. DVT prophylaxis (compression stockings/early ambulation). Report heavy vaginal bleeding, wound discharge, fever, or calf pain.
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Continuous monitoring for 2-4 hours. Check uterine tone and vaginal bleeding every 15 minutes. Monitor vitals. Assess neonatal APGAR. Input/output monitoring.

entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours. Pre-anesthesia assessment. Ultrasound abdomen confirmation. Blood grouping and cross-match. Prophylactic antibiotics at induction.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Start clear liquids after 6 hours, advance to soft diet next day. Early ambulation. Shoulder tip pain (referred from CO2) is normal and transient. Wound care for port sites. Resume normal activity in 1-2 weeks. Report persistent abdominal pain, jaundice, fever, or wound discharge.
entries.consentType: Written informed consent (includes consent for conversion to open)
preProcedureRequirements: CBC, LFT, coagulation profile, blood grouping
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor in recovery for 4-6 hours. Check vitals every 15 minutes. Assess for abdominal distension and pain. Monitor drain output if placed.

entries.anesthesiaType: Local Anesthesia (dorsal penile nerve block) / General Anesthesia (pediatric)
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 4-6 hours (if GA planned). Informed consent. Assess for bleeding disorders. Local anesthetic preparation.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Apply petroleum jelly gauze dressing. Loose-fitting clothing. Sitz baths after 48 hours. Avoid strenuous activity for 2 weeks. Mild swelling and bruising is normal. Report excessive bleeding, persistent swelling, or signs of infection.
contraindications: Hypospadias or epispadias (foreskin needed for repair)
entries.consentType: Written informed consent (parental consent for minors)
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Observe for 1-2 hours for bleeding. Ensure voiding. Wound check at 48 hours and 1 week.

entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 6-8 hours. Pre-anesthesia assessment. CBC and coagulation profile. Stop aspirin/NSAIDs 7-10 days before. Inform about bleeding history.
entries.postProcedureInstructions: Soft/cold diet for 1-2 weeks (curd, ice cream, cold milk, mashed foods). Adequate hydration. Avoid hot, spicy, or rough foods. Mild ear pain is referred pain and normal. Report any bleeding (spitting blood), high fever, or inability to swallow fluids. Activity restriction for 2 weeks.
entries.consentType: Written informed consent (parental consent for pediatric patients)
entries.postProcedureMonitoring: Monitor in recovery for 2-4 hours. Observe for bleeding from tonsillar fossa. Ensure adequate oral intake before discharge. Pain management.
